thread.title: $50M awarded to modernize Anchorage port

author: @AKNewsNow
title: $50M awarded to modernize Anchorage port
text:
$50M awarded to modernize Anchorage port
ANCHORAGE, Alaska (KTUU) - Anchorage Mayor Suzanne LaFrance, along with local leaders of the Don Young Port of Alaska, celebrated the $50 million funding for the port modernization project on Friday.
The funding was [announced last week](https://www.alaskasnewssource.com/2024/11/15/feds-fund-104m-worth-alaska-port-improvements/) by the U.S. Department of Transportation.
The money will help finance the rebuilding of a cargo terminal at the port and is expected to free up more than $20 million in state funds to put towards the project.
Port director Steve Ribuffo, said the modernization project is moving ahead and likely to be finished in about five years.
“You are going to see a facility designed with a 70-year lifespan that is state of the industry and capable of handling any kind of cargo vessel that is sent here from anywhere,” Ribuffo said in a news conference Friday. “A facility that is built to survive this seismic environment, and last for 70 years, that is right-sized for the business that we know we’re going to have for the foreseeable future with space to grow, is what you’re going to see when we’re done.”
The Anchorage Assembly recently approved $180 million in revenue bonds to begin paying for the next phase of the project and increasing the surcharges that companies pay to ship goods to or from the port to help pay for those bonds.
